title: Functions as contracts deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Functions as contracts; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Functions as contracts: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Knowing a function's name and parameter count is enough to use it safely without understanding its contract.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Callers and tests need explicit input constraints, result meaning, effects, and failure rules; implementation can change while that contract remains stable.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Callers and tests need explicit input constraints, result meaning, effects, and failure rules; implementation can change while that contract remains stable.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Functions as contracts instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Callers and tests need explicit input constraints, result meaning, effects, and failure rules; implementation can change while that contract remains stable.
